response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a strong track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and cloud-native development, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The two most disqualifying gaps are the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency (a non-negotiable requirement) and no demonstrable background in C-based embedded firmware, kernel-level Linux development, or network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). His single C/C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 was for a desktop IDE, not embedded or networking contexts, and his embedded mention in the resume bio is not substantiated by relevant experience. The role requires deep specialization this candidate does not possess.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, and systems domains demonstrates strong general engineering maturity.
Exposure to Linux environments and low-latency/high-performance systems work (Sociomantic, DLang async I/O) shows some systems-level thinking.
Experience with REST APIs, CI/CD infrastructure, and open-source project ownership indicates solid engineering discipline and self-direction.
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency listed — this is a stated mandatory requirement and alone disqualifies the candidate.
No experience with embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, L2/L3 protocols, or any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC auth).

C programming appears only marginally in the resume (one role in 2014–2015); the candidate's primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js with no evidence of Linux kernel development or chip-level/embedded work.

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security protocol experience
Minimal C / no Linux kernel experience
